Container.Overview:
[b]AllDebrid-Client v1.4.2[/b] — Self-hosted torrent automation via AllDebrid.
Add magnet links or `.torrent` files through the Web UI, Jackett search, a watch folder, Sonarr/Radarr, or the REST API. AllDebrid-Client handles the full lifecycle: upload → poll → parallel unlock → aria2 download → notify.
[b]Features:[/b] • Full lifecycle: upload → poll → unlock (parallel) → aria2 → done → Discord • Built-in aria2 daemon — no separate container required (or use your own external aria2) • Live Downloads view in the sidebar — 1-second auto-refresh, progress bars, Pause/Resume/Remove • Quick speed-limit preset (Unlimited / 1 / 2 / 5 / 10 / 20 / 50 MB/s + Custom) • Jackett search with category + indexer filters and direct Add from results • Watch folder — drop .torrent or .magnet files for auto-import • Discord rich embeds for add / complete / error / partial / FlexGet / stats events • FlexGet v3 integration (schedule + manual trigger from the UI) • Sonarr / Radarr download-client integration with post-download import trigger • File filters — block by extension, keyword, or minimum size • SQLite (default, zero-config) or external PostgreSQL • Reporting webhooks, stats snapshots with retention, and JSON export • Database backups on a configurable schedule
[b]New in v1.4.0:[/b] • unlock_link calls are now parallel → 10-file torrent unlocked in ~0.6s instead of ~4s • aria2 dispatch poll: 5s → 1s (downloads start within ~1s of being queued) • Downloads sidebar view with real-time queue and speed-limit control
[b]Quick Setup:[/b] 1. Set paths for Config, Data, Watch, Processed, and Downloads 2. Open http://[IP]:8080 → Settings → enter AllDebrid API key → Save 3. Set Download client to aria2-builtin (built-in) or configure external aria2 RPC URL 4. Add magnets via UI, Jackett Search, or drop files in the Watch folder
[b]Network:[/b] Uses host network mode to avoid conflicts with Unraid shares (shfs).
Container.Description: Self-hosted torrent automation via AllDebrid. Parallel link unlocking, built-in or external aria2, live Downloads view with speed-limit control, Jackett search, Discord webhooks, FlexGet, Sonarr/Radarr, file filters, SQLite/PostgreSQL.

Config.Name: Config Folder
Config.Description: [REQUIRED] Stores config.json. Must be persistent across restarts.

Config.Name: Data Folder
Config.Description: [REQUIRED] Stores the SQLite database and automatic backups.

Config.Name: Watch Folder
Config.Description: Drop .torrent or .magnet files here for automatic import. Leave empty if unused.

Config.Name: Processed Folder
Config.Description: Torrent/magnet files are moved here after processing. Leave empty if unused.

Config.Name: Download Folder
Config.Description: [REQUIRED] Downloaded files land here. If using external aria2, this path must match aria2's download directory.

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: User ID for downloaded files. Must match the UID of the user running your other media containers (Sonarr, Radarr, Plex). Run 'id' in a terminal on the host to find the right value. Files will be owned by this UID so other containers can read and write them.

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID for downloaded files. Must match the GID of the group used by your other media containers. Run 'id' in a terminal on the host to find the right value.

Config.Name: Timezone
Config.Description: Container timezone — used for all timestamps in logs and Discord messages. Examples: Europe/Berlin, UTC, America/New_York.

Config.Name: Log Level
Config.Description: Logging verbosity: INFO for normal operation, DEBUG for troubleshooting.

Config.Name: Database Type
Config.Description: Leave empty for SQLite (default, no setup required). Set to 'postgres' to use an external PostgreSQL database — configure the connection in Settings → Database.

Config.Name: Config Path
Config.Description: Internal config file path. Do not change unless you have a specific reason.

Config.Name: DB Path
Config.Description: Internal SQLite database path. Do not change unless you have a specific reason.
